name: Pot Stickers (Huo Tip)
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces Cabbage (1/2 cup cooked and chopped)
1 tablespoon Tientsin Preserved Vegetable (rinsed and chopped)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------SEASONING-------------------------
1/4 teaspoon Salt
1/4 teaspoon Sugar
1 tablespoon Thin soy sauce
1 tablespoon Oyster sauce
1 tablespoon White wine
Dash of pepper
2 teaspoons Cornstarch
----------------------------DIP----------------------------
2 tablespoons Thin soy sauce
1 tablespoon Hot spiced oil
1 tablespoon White Vinegar
Combine flour and cold water in a large mixing bowl. Mix thoroughly to form dough. Cover with a damp cloth; let stand for 20 minutes.
Cook cabbage 2 minutes in boiling water. Rinse in cold water, and drain. Squeeze dry of all excess moisture. Chop fine; set aside.
Place the ground pork, chopped green onion, chopped ginger, and preserved vegetables together on a chopping board. Mix and chop with the cleaver for approximately 15 strokes.
Add seasoning and the cabbage to the pork mixture and mix thoroughly.
After dough has set for 20 minutes, knead it for 1 minute and then form into 2 to 3 rolls, approximately 1-1/2 inch in diameter. Cut each roll of dough into slices 1-1/2 inch thick.
With a small rolling pin or the heel of your hand, flatten each piece of dough into a thin patty, 3 inches in diameter. Place 1/4 tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each patty. Fold in half, and seal the edges by pressing firmly together with your fingers.
(Help ready-made skins to seal by applying egg white to the edges.)
Set each pot sticker on a platter, pressing firmly so as to form a flat base. Each pot sticker should be made to stand upright, rather than allowed to rest flat on its side.
Heat frying pan and add 2 tablespoons oil. On medium heat, pan-fry pot stickers. Cook on the base surface only. Pan-fry for 2 minutes, until the base side is nicely browned.
Add 1 cup chicken stock to the pot stickers. Cover and cook over medium heat for approximately 7 minutes, until most of the chicken stock is absorbed. Remove to platter.
Mix thin soy sauce, hot spiced oil, and white vinegar to use as condiment for Pot Stickers.
Pot sticker skins can be purchased at Chinese groceries.
SOURCE: Chopstick, Cleaver and Wok.
